reforms.description: The Polish government adopted measures in 2007 to modernize vocational education curricula and strengthen active labor market policies, increasing the proportion of registered unemployed enrolled in training programs from less than 5% in 2002 to nearly 20% in 2007, aiming to better align education with labor market needs.
reforms.package_name: Vocational Education and Labor Market Reforms
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving vocational education and training enhances the skills of the workforce, which supports productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The measures were adopted and have been in effect since 2007.
reforms.importance_rationale: These reforms significantly improve the alignment of education with labor market needs, but do not constitute a major overhaul of the system.
reforms.source_quote: In order to facilitate the modernisation of curricula in vocational programmes, the government adopted a number of measures in 2007.

reforms.description: The Polish government plans to strengthen the administrative capacity and human-resource base of relevant government agencies to improve the absorption of EU funds for transport infrastructure projects.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing administrative capacity will facilitate better management of EU funds, leading to improved infrastructure and economic development.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has announced plans to strengthen capacity but has not yet implemented them.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it aims to improve the efficiency of fund absorption but does not represent a major structural change.
reforms.source_quote: There are plans to strengthen the administrative capacity and human-resource base of relevant government agencies.

reforms.description: The Polish government is working to streamline public procurement regulations to reduce bureaucratic delays in infrastructure projects.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Streamlining procurement processes can enhance efficiency and speed up infrastructure development, contributing to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government is actively working on this reform but it has not yet been implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it addresses inefficiencies in public procurement but does not represent a major overhaul.
reforms.source_quote: The reform is focused on streamlining certain procedures, notably those that often led to cancelling tenders and rejecting bids.

reforms.description: The Polish government adopted the Motorway Construction Programme in 1993 and the Act on Toll Motorways in 1994, allowing for licensing arrangements for motorway construction, which aimed to mobilize private investment in transport infrastructure.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Mobilizing private investment in infrastructure projects enhances economic development and improves transport networks.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme and act have been in effect since their adoption in 1994.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a major structural change in how infrastructure projects are financed and executed.
reforms.source_quote: The adoption by the government of the Motorway Construction Programme in 1993 and by the Parliament of the Act on Toll Motorways in October 1994 allowed the use of licensing arrangements for the construction of motorways.

reforms.description: In mid-2003, Poland decided to prepare a special PPP law to stimulate public-sector investments with private participation, aiming to reduce risks associated with previous legal frameworks.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Developing a PPP law can enhance public investment efficiency and attract private capital, fostering economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The law was prepared and implemented in 2005.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is a significant structural change aimed at improving public investment frameworks.
reforms.source_quote: In mid-2003, it was decided to render the legislative system more favourable by preparing a special PPP law.
